topic: Eclipse trilemma — citation fix from formula_runtime.json to status_history.json (EXP-269)
trigger_note: EXP-269 integrated a single targeted-substring patch in the Geometric horn of §4.2.7. The patch replaces a fragile/incorrect citation (formula_runtime.json) with the correct file (status_history.json) for the Solar/Moon Angular Diameter domains, and adds a verification timestamp.
recommendation_summary: The citation correction does what it claims and removes the H6 residual hole from EXP-254. One moderate hole remains in the new wording: 'currently 39 scored domains' contradicts the §4.1 audit (just rewritten via EXP-270) which carefully distinguishes 28 scored + 11 pending = 39 total. A defender will quote both lines verbatim. Recommend a small substring edit to align §4.2.7 with §4.1.
our_argument_summary: §4.2.7 runs the August 12, 2026 eclipse through the dome's own published parameters (h_Sun=5,733 km, h_Moon=2,534 km, NASA Besselian angular sizes) and shows the dome geometry produces a totality zone ~1.3 km wide vs. the observed 294 km path. The trilemma names three concessions the dome must accept (parameters wrong / globe geometry borrowed / non-occultation aetheric mechanism with unexplained station localization). The citation in the Geometric horn now points to status_history.json instead of formula_runtime.json — a fix EXP-269 introduced after EXP-254 integration left a residual citation defect.

patch_review.anchor_text_after: Adjusting the angular sizes contradicts the dome's own match to observed apparent diameters in the dome's live-power dashboard (the Solar Angular Diameter and Moon Angular Diameter Variation domains, recorded in status_history.json — verified live 2026-04-27, currently 39 scored domains).
patch_review.parallel_formula_runtime_audit: The §4.2.7 prose still cites formula_runtime.json once earlier — 'formula_runtime.json publishes six formulas, none of them an eclipse mechanism'. That citation is correct (it cites formula_runtime.json for what formula_runtime.json actually contains). No further citation surgery needed.

holes_found.description: The new wording asserts 'currently 39 scored domains' but §4.1 (rewritten via EXP-270, integrated 2026-04-28T05:18Z, six hours after EXP-269 was queued) carefully distinguishes 28 scored + 11 pending = 39 total. §4.1 specifically says: "the dome's underlying status_history.json exposes a total_domains: 39 count (28 scored + 11 pending)" and "100.0% / 28 of 28 scored domains passing." §4.2.7 now contradicts §4.1 in our own document. A dome defender will quote both lines side by side: 'Your §4.1 says 28 scored, your §4.2.7 says 39 scored — pick one.' This is exactly the kind of internal-inconsistency rebuttal we use against the dome; we should not commit it ourselves.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'currently 39 scored domains' with 'currently 39 domains in the registry (28 scored, 11 pending), per the dome's own audit accounting' — or, simpler and tighter, 'currently 39 domains in the registry.' Either avoids the 28-vs-39 inconsistency. Note: this is a substring tweak only; the citation file (status_history.json), the domain names (Solar Angular Diameter, Moon Angular Diameter Variation), and the verification timestamp (2026-04-27) all stay.

holes_found.description: Sole-source citation. The patched text relies entirely on the analyst's 2026-04-27 live-fetch verification. The dashboard live-updates daily and the registry has already grown 28→39 in ten days (per §4.1); a future reader checking the cited file might find a 40+ domain count and question the '39' number. The verification timestamp anchors the snapshot, but the text doesn't flag that the count is volatile.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional. If you take H1's second wording ('currently 39 domains in the registry'), consider appending '(count drifts upward as new prospective domains are added; verified 2026-04-27).' Not blocking — the timestamp already does most of this work. Mention only because §4.1 explicitly notes the σ-card count drifts, and the same caveat applies to the domain count.

holes_found.description: EXP-270's rewrite of §4.1 happened AFTER EXP-269 was queued — so EXP-269's author had no opportunity to coordinate phrasing with §4.1's newly-integrated 28/11/39 split. This is the upstream cause of H1 and is more a coordination observation than a defect in EXP-269 itself.
holes_found.recommendation: Process note for the decider: when two prose-touching patches land within ~24h of each other on adjacent sections (here EXP-269 on §4.2.7 then EXP-270 on §4.1, both within 8 hours), the integration step should run a quick pairwise consistency scan on shared vocabulary. The 28/11/39 phrasing in §4.1 is the canonical accounting; §4.2.7 should match it.

stronger_arguments.description: The Geometric horn could borrow strength from §4.1's audit: rather than just naming the two domains, the citation could note that BOTH Solar Angular Diameter and Moon Angular Diameter Variation are among the 28 scored domains the dome reports as 100% passing — making the trilemma's squeeze tighter. Suggested wording: 'Adjusting the angular sizes contradicts the dome's own claim that its Solar Angular Diameter and Moon Angular Diameter Variation domains pass at 100% (status_history.json, 2026-04-27 snapshot, 28-of-28 scored).' This is a wording suggestion, not a required fix; it strengthens the rhetorical move by quantifying the WIN-on-the-line.

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-269 correctly identifies and fixes a real citation defect (H6 from the EXP-254 review). formula_runtime.json contains six formula records, not domain-level scoring data; status_history.json is where the scored-domain entries live. The fix removes a citation that points readers to a file that does not contain the cited content — a defender would have caught this and used it. The verification timestamp is a methodological improvement over the original 'rows 23 and 28' framing, which was rejected for fragility against live-updating row counts.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_undermine_section: The structural argument of the Geometric horn is unchanged: adjusting angular sizes breaks an existing dome WIN. The new citation backs that argument more accurately. The only residual issue is the '39 scored' wording vs. §4.1's 28-scored framing — which is a cosmetic/consistency fix, not a structural problem.

citations_verified: status_history.json contains Solar Angular Diameter and Moon Angular Diameter Variation entries — claimed by analyst live-fetch 2026-04-27; cross-confirmed by §4.1 prose which independently references both file and the 39-domain count.
formula_runtime.json contains six formula records FORM-V51-001..006 — verified by §4.1 audit ('six published equations') and by independent reference at offset 13325 of part4.html.
citations_unchecked: Live web fetch of status_history.json was not re-attempted in this review; review relies on EXP-269 author's 2026-04-27 live-fetch and the consistent §4.1 prose. If the registry has grown beyond 39 since 2026-04-27, the '39' number will read stale (see H2).

cross_section_consistency.resolution_recommended: Align §4.2.7 with §4.1 (the audit is the canonical accounting).

deeper_analysis: EXP-269 is a textbook minor-followup patch: it fixes a single residual citation defect that the original integration left in place, with no structural change to the argument. The patch reads as one substring replacement, anchor present exactly once, expected occurrences honored. The integrity of the fix itself is high. The one moderate finding (H1) is not a flaw in the patch — it is a flaw in the COORDINATION between EXP-269 and EXP-270, which were authored as independent patches but landed within hours of each other and now disagree on the registry-domain accounting in adjacent sections. The fix is small (substring tweak in the same anchor block EXP-269 already touched). Considering the other side: the dome defender's strongest counter would not be 'the citation is wrong' (it's now right) but 'your own §4.1 says 28 scored and your §4.2.7 says 39 scored — your audit can't even count.' That is the only opening of consequence created by this patch. Closing it is a 30-character edit.
recommended_followups: Open a new ISS for the §4.2.7 '39 scored' → '28 scored / 39 total' alignment with §4.1. Suggest combining with H2 timestamp/volatility footnote in the same patch.

(Decider awareness, not a patch) When two prose-touching patches land within ~24h of each other on adjacent sections, run a pairwise vocabulary-consistency scan as part of the integration check. EXP-269 + EXP-270 are the case that prompted this.
